posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked:
• 2015 Roses de Jeanne / Cédric Bouchard Champagne Blanc de Noirs Côte de Val Vilaine - France, Champagne (4/13/2020)
    So freaking delicious right now. Deep apple and plum fruit, wheat and roasted corn accents and a long, rich finish. Stunningly good, with a long life ahead.

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: D@vid K@ntr0witz
posts.cooked: I agree. It’s a great vintage for this wine.
love this wine. probably my favorite sparkling producer out there for the combo of deliciousness and price point

posts.name: John Vizuete
posts.cooked: Very excited about my bottle in the mail, thanks for the note, David. Apart from the big houses, how does Bouchard stack up against other players at similar price points? I think of Chartogne Taillet, Egly Ouriet, Ulysses Colin, and Prevost in the same ballpark. How would you describe Bouchard’s house style?

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Apart from the big houses, how does Bouchard stack up against other players at similar price points? I think of Chartogne Taillet, Egly Ouriet, Ulysses Colin, and Prevost in the same ballpark. How would you describe Bouchard’s house style?

Cédric really operates entirely on his own terms. Yields are around 25 hl/ha, lower than many top domaines of the Côte de Nuits to say nothing of the average in Champagne, France’s highest yielding AOC. Vinification in in stainless steel, with neither chaptalization or dosage. The prise de mousse is slow. And the wines finish up with four rather than six bars of pressure, so the mousse is very subtle. On cork, Cédric’s champagnes develop more like still wines, as the Maillard reaction-driven interactions between amino acids from sur lattes aging and sugar from dosage are not really part of the equation. The result of all this is very vinous, concentrated and intense Champagnes that combine remarkable depth of fruit and mid-palate volume with lively acids, complemented by the lower pressure mousse I already mentioned.

I have been tasting through some of his new releases and actually did a video about Cédric here: William Kelley on Instagram: "Cédric Bouchard produces some of my favorite wines in the world. In this video, I talk about what makes them special."

And I did a long essay on him last year here: Robert Parker Wine Advocate - Unbiased Wine Reviews

posts.name: Subu Ramachandran
posts.cooked:
Great notes on Cedric Bouchard, William, enjoyed the video as well!

I opened a Côte de Bachelin, 2015 on Sunday. Smashing wine!

posts.name: Corentin C.
posts.cooked: Thank you for the note on Cedric Bouchard.

Great information William, and apologies for the delay - I had the notifications off

posts.name: Kelly Walker
posts.cooked: The 2015 Vilaine is a wine I have served some of my geek friends several times over the past 6 months. I get that raised eyebrow and smile. Says it all.

posts.name: Dennis Atick
posts.cooked: I really like this Champagne. Wish I could still find it for at or under $60. Those days seem to be over.

suggested_topics.title: What is Verlander's favorite wine?
suggested_topics.excerpt:
Verlander finds bottle of his favorite wine waiting at his locker after elusive 1st win with Giants 
The story is everywhere but I have not seen the name of the wine.

suggested_topics.title: Aged Barolo - 2001 Bruno Giacosa
suggested_topics.excerpt:
I have consistently had negative experiences with Barolo from auction that is more than 10 years old. Even with 2013 and 2010 they taste and smell oxidized. 
Last night, I opened a 2001 Bruno Giacosa Falletto di Serralun…
